content:
Overall, Welty’s use of symbolism makes Phoenix’s journey feel meaningful beyond just getting medicine. It reflects a lifetime of strength, love, and sacrifice, which makes her character so memorable.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 9, 2025 9:19AM
Katherine Moreno
Hey, Ashley . I really like how you explained the symbolism in A Worn Path, especially how Phoenix’s name connects to the mythological bird. That comparison makes her journey even more meaningful because, just like the phoenix, she continues to push forward despite hardships. Her endurance and determination stand out the most in the story, showing that she is willing to go through anything for the sake of her grandson.

The “worn path” itself is also such a powerful symbol. It represents all the struggles she has faced before and continues to face. The thorn bushes, logs, and even her old age make her journey even more difficult, yet she keeps going without hesitation. The quote, “Seems like there are chains about my feet, time I get this far,” really stood out to me because it shows how exhausting her journey is, yet she refuses to stop. It reminds me of how people in real life push through their struggles, especially when they have someone depending on them.
